description:
You are acting as a 2nd-line support engineer who has just finished gathering a small log bundle.  
  The bundle is located at:

    /home/user/diagnostics/collect-logs.log

  Your next step is to create a SHA-256 checksum file for this bundle so the customer can later
  prove the archive has not been tampered with.

  1. Generate the SHA-256 checksum of /home/user/diagnostics/collect-logs.log.  
  2. Store the result in a new file called:

       /home/user/diagnostics/collect-logs.sha256

  3. The .sha256 file **must contain exactly one line** in the canonical
     “sha256sum” output format:

       <64-character-hex-digest><two spaces>collect-logs.log\n

     • There must be exactly two space characters between the digest and the
       filename.  
     • Only the basename “collect-logs.log” (no leading path) must appear
       after the two spaces.  
     • The line must end with a single newline character (ASCII 0x0A).  
     • No additional lines, headers, or trailing whitespace are allowed.

  4. After creating the .sha256 file, display its contents in the terminal so
     the requester can visually confirm the result.

  Nothing else needs to be printed to the console besides the normal command
  output that shows the single checksum line.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing state -->
  Directory            : /home/user/diagnostics
  Pre-existing file     : /home/user/diagnostics/collect-logs.log
  Content of log file   : exactly three bytes “abc” (0x61 0x62 0x63), no trailing newline.
  
  <!-- Expected artefact after agent actions -->
  File to be created    : /home/user/diagnostics/collect-logs.sha256
  Exact file content    : 
    ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad␠␠collect-logs.log\n
  
  (The 64-character digest above is the SHA-256 hash of the literal bytes “abc”.  
   The “␠␠” indicates two ASCII space characters; “\n” is a single LF.)
name: task_000000_4737fa57
